Our analysis reveals two key challenges: (i) fine-tuning MLLMs with reasoning chains for classification is more challenging than direct label supervision, since the model must optimize complex reasoning sequences before label prediction; and (ii) mismatches in reasoning patterns between supervision signals and fine-tuned MLLMs lead to a trade-off between semantic richness (informative but harder to optimize) and optimization efficiency (easier to optimize but less informative). To address these issues, we propose RD-MLDG (Reasoning-Driven Multimodal LLM for Domain Generalization), a framework with two components: (i) MTCT (Multi-Task Cross-Training), which introduces an additional direct classification pathway to guide reasoning supervision; and (ii) SARR (Self-Aligned Reasoning Regularization), which preserves the semantic richness of reasoning chains while mitigating reasoning-pattern mismatches via iterative self-labeling. Experiments on standard DomainBed datasets (PACS, VLCS, OfficeHome, TerraInc) demonstrate that RD-MLDG achieves state-of-the-art performances, highlighting reasoning as a promising complementary signal for robust out-of-domain generalization.

AI.tldr: 本研究提出了一种名为RD-MLDG的框架，通过利用多模态大语言模型（MLLMs）的推理能力来解决深度学习中的领域泛化（DG）问题，并在DomainBed-Reasoning数据集上取得了最先进的性能。
AI.motivation: 现有的领域泛化方法主要关注视觉特征不变性，但忽略了多模态大语言模型的推理能力。本研究旨在探索通过构建推理链来驱动图像分类，以应对领域转移带来的挑战。
AI.method: 作者构建了DomainBed-Reasoning数据集，并提出了RD-MLDG框架，包含两个主要组件：MTCT（多任务交叉训练），通过引入直接分类通路来指导推理监督；SARR（自对齐推理正则化），通过迭代自标注来保留推理链的语义丰富性并缓解推理模式不匹配的问题。
AI.result: 实验表明，直接使用推理链进行微调比直接标签监督更具挑战性，并且推理模式的匹配度会影响模型性能。RD-MLDG框架在PACS、VLCS、OfficeHome、TerraInc等标准DomainBed数据集上取得了最先进的性能。
AI.conclusion: 推理可以作为一种有希望的互补信号，用于实现鲁棒的领域外泛化。RD-MLDG框架有效利用了MLLMs的推理能力，克服了直接监督推理的挑战，并在领域泛化任务上取得了优异的成果。

title: Structured Prompt Optimization for Few-Shot Text Classification via Semantic Alignment in Latent Space
summary: This study addresses the issues of semantic entanglement, unclear label structure, and insufficient feature representation in few-shot text classification, and proposes an optimization framework based on structured prompts to enhance semantic understanding and task adaptation under low-resource conditions. The framework first uses a pretrained language model to encode the input text and obtain basic semantic representations. It then introduces structured prompts composed of multi-dimensional semantic factors and integrates them with text features through a learnable combination mechanism, which forms task-related representations with clear boundaries in the latent space. To further strengthen the consistency between text representations and label semantics, the method constructs a structured label embedding matrix and employs a cross-space alignment mechanism to ensure stable matching between textual features and label attributes. In addition, the model applies prompt orthogonality constraints and a joint optimization objective to maintain independence across different semantic factors in the prompts, allowing the structured prompts to provide transparent and controllable guidance for classification decisions. Three types of sensitivity experiments, including learning rate sensitivity, prompt length sensitivity, and data scale sensitivity, are designed to evaluate the stability and robustness of the framework under different conditions. Experimental results show that the proposed structured prompt optimization framework effectively alleviates semantic conflicts and label ambiguity in few-shot text classification. It significantly improves performance on accuracy, precision, recall, and AUC, and demonstrates strong cross-task applicability.

AI.tldr: 本研究提出了一种基于结构化提示的优化框架，用于解决少样本文本分类中的语义纠缠、标签结构不清和特征表示不足的问题，通过多维度语义因子和结构化标签嵌入来增强模型在低资源条件下的语义理解和任务适应能力。
AI.motivation: 少样本文本分类面临语义纠缠、标签结构不清晰以及特征表示不足等挑战。
AI.method: 该框架利用预训练语言模型编码文本，引入由多维度语义因子组成的结构化提示，并结合可学习的组合机制形成任务相关的表示。同时，构建结构化标签嵌入矩阵，并利用跨空间对齐机制匹配文本特征与标签属性。此外，模型还采用提示正交性约束和联合优化目标来保持语义因子的独立性。
AI.result: 实验结果表明，该结构化提示优化框架有效缓解了语义冲突和标签歧义，显著提高了准确率、精确率、召回率和AUC，并展现出强大的跨任务适用性。
AI.conclusion: 提出的结构化提示优化框架能够有效提升少样本文本分类的性能，并在不同条件下表现出稳定性和鲁棒性。

title: BuildAnyPoint: 3D Building Structured Abstraction from Diverse Point Clouds
summary: We introduce BuildAnyPoint, a novel generative framework for structured 3D building reconstruction from point clouds with diverse distributions, such as those captured by airborne LiDAR and Structure-from-Motion. To recover artist-created building abstraction in this highly underconstrained setting, we capitalize on the role of explicit 3D generative priors in autoregressive mesh generation. Specifically, we design a Loosely Cascaded Diffusion Transformer (Loca-DiT) that initially recovers the underlying distribution from noisy or sparse points, followed by autoregressively encapsulating them into compact meshes. We first formulate distribution recovery as a conditional generation task by training latent diffusion models conditioned on input point clouds, and then tailor a decoder-only transformer for conditional autoregressive mesh generation based on the recovered point clouds. Our method delivers substantial qualitative and quantitative improvements over prior building abstraction methods. Furthermore, the effectiveness of our approach is evidenced by the strong performance of its recovered point clouds on building point cloud completion benchmarks, which exhibit improved surface accuracy and distribution uniformity.

AI.tldr: BuildAnyPoint 是一个新颖的生成框架，用于从具有不同分布的点云（如航空 LiDAR 和运动恢复结构捕获的）中进行结构化 3D 建筑重建。该方法利用显式的 3D 生成先验，通过 Loosely Cascaded Diffusion Transformer (Loca-DiT) 进行自回归网格生成，首先恢复点云的潜在分布，然后将其封装成紧凑的网格。
AI.motivation: 在高度欠约束的情况下，从具有不同分布的点云中恢复艺术家创建的建筑抽象。
AI.method: 使用 Loosely Cascaded Diffusion Transformer (Loca-DiT)，这是一个两阶段的框架。第一阶段将分布恢复作为条件生成任务，训练基于输入点云的潜在扩散模型。第二阶段使用解码器专用的 Transformer 进行条件自回归网格生成，以恢复的点云为基础。
AI.result: 在定性和定量方面均优于现有的建筑抽象方法。恢复的点云在建筑点云补全基准测试中表现出更强的性能，表面精度和分布均匀性均有所提高。
AI.conclusion: BuildAnyPoint 能够有效地从多样化的点云中进行结构化 3D 建筑重建，并能在挑战性的条件下恢复建筑的艺术抽象。所提出的 Loca-DiT 架构在分布恢复和网格生成方面都取得了显著的进步。

title: From Static Benchmarks to Dynamic Protocol: Agent-Centric Text Anomaly Detection for Evaluating LLM Reasoning
summary: The evaluation of large language models (LLMs) has predominantly relied on static datasets, which offer limited scalability and fail to capture the evolving reasoning capabilities of recent models. To overcome these limitations, we propose an agent-centric benchmarking paradigm that moves beyond static datasets by introducing a dynamic protocol in which autonomous agents iteratively generate, validate, and solve problems. Within this protocol, a teacher agent generates candidate problems, an orchestrator agent rigorously verifies their validity and guards against adversarial attacks, and a student agent attempts to solve the validated problems. An invalid problem is revised by the teacher agent until it passes validation. If the student correctly solves the problem, the orchestrator prompts the teacher to generate more challenging variants. Consequently, the benchmark scales in difficulty automatically as more capable agents are substituted into any role, enabling progressive evaluation of large language models without manually curated datasets. Adopting text anomaly detection as our primary evaluation format, which demands cross-sentence logical inference and resists pattern-matching shortcuts, we demonstrate that this protocol systematically exposes corner-case reasoning errors that conventional benchmarks fail to reveal. We further advocate evaluating systems along several complementary axes including cross-model pairwise performance and progress between the initial and orchestrator-finalized problems. By shifting the focus from fixed datasets to dynamic protocols, our approach offers a sustainable direction for evaluating ever-evolving language models and introduces a research agenda centered on the co-evolution of agent-centric benchmarks.

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种新的LLM评估范式，即以代理为中心的基准测试，通过让自主代理迭代生成、验证和解决问题来克服静态数据集的局限性。该方法能够自动扩展基准难度，并能系统地暴露传统基准难以发现的推理错误。
AI.motivation: 现有的LLM评估方法依赖于静态数据集，这些数据集扩展性有限，且无法捕捉模型不断发展的推理能力。需要一种更具动态性和可扩展性的评估方法。
AI.method: 提出一种以代理为中心的基准测试协议：教师代理生成问题，协调代理验证问题并防御攻击，学生代理解决问题。无效问题会被教师代理修改直至通过验证。学生解决问题后，会生成更难的版本。评估格式为文本异常检测。
AI.result: 该协议能够自动扩展基准难度，系统地暴露传统基准未能揭示的推理错误。通过评估跨模型表现和问题难度变化，可以更全面地衡量模型能力。
AI.conclusion: 提出了一种可持续的LLM评估方向，即从固定数据集转向动态协议，并开启了围绕代理中心基准协同演进的研究议程。

title: DLEBench: Evaluating Small-scale Object Editing Ability for Instruction-based Image Editing Model
summary: Significant progress has been made in the field of Instruction-based Image Editing Models (IIEMs). However, while these models demonstrate plausible adherence to instructions and strong reasoning ability on current benchmarks, their ability to edit small objects remains underexplored, despite its importance for precise local editing and refining details in both real and generated images. In this paper, we introduce DeepLookEditBench (DLEBench), the first benchmark dedicated to assessing the abilities of IIEMs in editing small-scale objects. Specifically, we construct a challenging testbed comprising 1889 samples across seven instruction types. In these samples, target objects occupy only 1%-10% of the image area, covering complex scenarios such as partial occlusion and multi-object editing. To ensure robust evaluation on this benchmark, we propose an evaluation protocol with refined score rubrics to minimize subjectivity and ambiguity in two criteria: Instruction Following and Visual Consistency. This protocol also introduces a dual-mode evaluation framework (Tool-driven and Oracle-guided Modes) addressing the misalignment between LMM-as-a-Judge and human judgements on DLEBench. Empirical results on 10 IIEMs reveal significant performance gaps in small-scale object editing, highlighting the need for specialized benchmarks to advance this ability.

AI.tldr: 本文提出了DeepLookEditBench（DLEBench），这是第一个专门用于评估指令驱动图像编辑模型（IIEMs）在编辑小目标对象方面的基准。该基准包含1889个样本，覆盖七种指令类型，目标对象仅占图像面积的1%-10%。为确保评估的鲁棒性，本文还提出了一种改进的评估协议，包括指令遵循和视觉一致性两个标准，并引入了工具驱动和Oracle引导的双模式评估框架。实验表明，现有IIEMs在小目标对象编辑方面存在显著的性能差距。
AI.motivation: 尽管指令驱动的图像编辑模型（IIEMs）在处理一般性指令和推理方面取得了显著进展，但它们在编辑小目标对象方面的能力仍未得到充分探索，而这对于精确的局部编辑和细节调整至关重要。
AI.method: 1. 提出了DeepLookEditBench（DLEBench），一个包含1889个样本、七种指令类型的基准，专门用于测试IIEMs在编辑占图像面积1%-10%的小目标对象的能力。 2. 设计了一个改进的评估协议，包含指令遵循和视觉一致性两个标准，以减少评估的主观性和模糊性。 3. 引入了一个工具驱动和Oracle引导的双模式评估框架，以解决语言模型作为裁判与人类判断之间的不一致问题。 4. 在10个IIEMs上进行了实证评估。
AI.result: 在DLEBench基准上的实证评估揭示了10个IIEMs在小目标对象编辑方面存在显著的性能差距，表明现有模型在该能力上不足。
AI.conclusion: DLEBench是首个专注于IIEMs小目标对象编辑能力的基准，其结果表明，现有的IIEMs在该领域存在重大挑战，需要专门的研究和开发来提升其能力。

title: Acceleration-Based Control of Fixed-Wing UAVs for Guidance Applications
summary: Acceleration-commanded guidance laws (e.g., proportional navigation) are attractive for high-level decision making, but their direct deployment on fixed-wing UAVs is challenging because accelerations are not directly actuated and must be realized through attitude and thrust under flight-envelope constraints. This paper presents an acceleration-level outer-loop control framework that converts commanded tangential and normal accelerations into executable body-rate and normalized thrust commands compatible with mainstream autopilots (e.g., PX4/APM). For the normal channel, we derive an engineering mapping from the desired normal acceleration to roll- and pitch-rate commands that regulate the direction and magnitude of the lift vector under small-angle assumptions. For the tangential channel, we introduce an energy-based formulation inspired by total energy control and identify an empirical thrust-energy acceleration relationship directly from flight data, avoiding explicit propulsion modeling or thrust bench calibration. We further discuss priority handling between normal and tangential accelerations under saturation and non-level maneuvers. Extensive real-flight experiments on a VTOL fixed-wing platform demonstrate accurate acceleration tracking and enable practical implementation of proportional navigation using only body-rate and normalized thrust interfaces.

AI.tldr: 该论文提出了一种用于固定翼无人机的加速度级外环控制框架，可以将期望的法向和切向加速度指令转换为自动驾驶仪可执行的指令，并展示了在实际飞行中实现比例导航的可行性。
AI.motivation: 现有加速度指令制导律（如比例导航）直接应用于固定翼无人机面临挑战，因为加速度不能直接驱动，需要通过姿态和推力在飞行包线约束下实现。本研究旨在克服这一障碍，实现加速度级控制。
AI.method: 提出了一个加速度级外环控制框架。对于法向通道，推导了期望法向加速度到滚转角和俯仰角速率指令的工程映射。对于切向通道，引入了基于能量的公式，并从飞行数据中直接确定了推力-能量加速度关系。讨论了饱和和非平动情况下的优先级处理。
AI.result: 框架能够将期望的法向和切向加速度转换为兼容主流自动驾驶仪的指令。实际飞行实验证明了准确的加速度跟踪能力。
AI.conclusion: 该加速度级外环控制框架成功实现了固定翼无人机上的比例导航，仅通过本体速率和归一化推力接口即可实现，并具有实际应用价值。

title: Divide and Conquer: Accelerating Diffusion-Based Large Language Models via Adaptive Parallel Decoding
summary: Diffusion-based large language models (dLLMs) have shown promising performance across various reasoning tasks, establishing themselves as an alternative to autoregressive large language models (LLMs). Unlike autoregressive LLMs that generate one token per step based on all previous tokens, dLLMs theoretically enable parallel generation of multiple tokens at each decoding step. However, recent dLLMs still favor one-token-per-step generation in practice, as directly decoding multiple masked tokens often leads to degraded generation quality and stability. This reveals a substantial gap between the theoretical parallelism and practical performance of dLLMs. To bridge this gap, we introduce an adaptive parallel decoding approach, namely DiCo, which features a three-phase divide-and-conquer paradigm to unleash the inherent parallelism of dLLMs. During the Divide phase, DiCo first explores the input masked sequence and identifies masked tokens as seed tokens, which are then expanded to construct a set of local clusters. During the Conquer phase, DiCo performs parallel decoding across different local clusters constructed in the Divide phase. The divide-and-conquer process repeatedly alternates between the Divide and Conquer phases until convergence. During the Finalize phase, DiCo decodes the remaining few masked tokens using an effective fine-grained compound decoding scheme to finalize the generation. Extensive experiments demonstrate that DiCo can achieve significant inference speedups while maintaining competitive generation quality.

AI.tldr: 提出了一种名为DiCo的自适应并行解码方法，通过三阶段的“分而治之”范式，克服了扩散式大语言模型（dLLMs）在实际应用中无法充分发挥并行生成优势的问题，在保证生成质量的同时显著提升了推理速度。
AI.motivation: 现有扩散式大语言模型（dLLMs）在实际应用中仍倾向于逐个 token 生成，未能充分发挥其理论上的并行生成能力，导致实际性能与理论潜力之间存在差距。
AI.method: 提出了一种名为DiCo的自适应并行解码方法，包含三个阶段：1. Divide（划分）阶段：识别 seed tokens 并构建局部聚类；2. Conquer（征服）阶段：在局部聚类之间并行解码，并交替进行Divide和Conquer直至收敛；3. Finalize（最终确定）阶段：使用精细的复合解码方案解码剩余 token。
AI.result: 实验证明，DiCo 在显著提升推理速度的同时，能够保持与现有方法相当的生成质量。
AI.conclusion: DiCo 成功弥合了 dLLMs 理论并行能力与实际应用之间的差距，通过创新的“分而治之”解码策略，实现了 dLLMs 的高效并行推理。

title: 3D Modality-Aware Pre-training for Vision-Language Model in MRI Multi-organ Abnormality Detection
summary: Vision-language models (VLMs) show strong potential for complex diagnostic tasks in medical imaging. However, applying VLMs to multi-organ medical imaging introduces two principal challenges: (1) modality-specific vision-language alignment and (2) cross-modal feature fusion. In this work, we propose MedMAP, a Medical Modality-Aware Pretraining framework that enhances vision-language representation learning in 3D MRI. MedMAP comprises a modality-aware vision-language alignment stage and a fine-tuning stage for multi-organ abnormality detection. During the pre-training stage, the modality-aware encoders implicitly capture the joint modality distribution and improve alignment between visual and textual representations. We then fine-tune the pre-trained vision encoders (while keeping the text encoder frozen) for downstream tasks. To this end, we curated MedMoM-MRI3D, comprising 7,392 3D MRI volume-report pairs spanning twelve MRI modalities and nine abnormalities tailored for various 3D medical analysis tasks. Extensive experiments on MedMoM-MRI3D demonstrate that MedMAP significantly outperforms existing VLMs in 3D MRI-based multi-organ abnormality detection. Our code is available at https://github.com/RomantiDr/MedMAP.

AI.tldr: 本文提出了MedMAP框架，用于增强3D MRI医学影像中视觉-语言模型的学习能力，解决了多器官医学影像的模态特定对齐和跨模态特征融合问题，并在新数据集MedMoM-MRI3D上实现了显著的性能提升。
AI.motivation: 现有视觉-语言模型（VLMs）在处理多器官医学影像时面临模态特定对齐和跨模态特征融合的挑战。
AI.method: 提出MedMAP框架，包含模态感知视觉-语言对齐预训练阶段和针对多器官异常检测的微调阶段。预训练阶段通过模态感知编码器隐式捕捉联合模态分布，改善视觉和文本表示的对齐。随后，对预训练的视觉编码器进行微调（文本编码器保持冻结）。
AI.result: 在精心构建的MedMoM-MRI3D数据集（包含7,392个3D MRI体积-报告对，涵盖十二种MRI模态和九种异常）上进行的广泛实验表明，MedMAP在3D MRI多器官异常检测任务上显著优于现有VLMs。
AI.conclusion: MedMAP框架能够有效提升3D MRI医学影像的视觉-语言表示学习，并在多器官异常检测任务中取得优异性能。

title: RUMAD: Reinforcement-Unifying Multi-Agent Debate
summary:
Multi-agent debate (MAD) systems leverage collective intelligence to enhance reasoning capabilities, yet existing approaches struggle to simultaneously optimize accuracy, consensus formation, and computational efficiency. Static topology methods lack adaptability to task complexity variations, while external LLM-based coordination risks introducing privileged knowledge that compromises debate neutrality. This work presents RUMAD (Reinforcement-Unifying Multi-Agent Debate), a novel framework that formulates dynamic communication topology control in MAD as a reinforcement learning (RL) problem.
  RUMAD employs a content-agnostic observation scheme that captures high-level debate dynamics avoiding access to raw agent reasoning content. RUMAD uses a multi-objective reward to model solution quality, cohesion and efficiency. A PPO-trained controller dynamically adjusts edge weights in the communication graph, while a dual-threshold mechanism enables fine-grained control over both agent activation and information visibility.
  Experimental evaluation across MMLU, GSM8K, and GPQA benchmarks demonstrates that RUMAD achieves substantial efficiency gains, reducing token costs by over 80\%, while still improving reasoning accuracy compared to single LLM model and multiple MAD baselines. Notably, RUMAD trained exclusively on MMLU exhibits robust zero-shot generalization to out-of-domain (OOD) tasks, indicating that the learned communication strategies capture task-independent principles of effective multi-agent coordination. These results establish RUMAD as a efficient and robust approach for deploying multi-agent reasoning application with practical resource constraints.

AI.tldr: 本研究提出了一种名为RUMAD的新型多智能体辩论（MAD）框架，通过强化学习动态控制通信拓扑，以在准确性、共识形成和计算效率之间取得平衡，并在多个基准测试中实现了显著的效率提升和准确性改进。
AI.motivation: 现有MAD系统在优化准确性、共识形成和计算效率方面存在挑战。静态拓扑缺乏适应性，而外部LLM协调可能引入偏见。研究旨在开发一种能够动态调整通信拓扑以应对不同任务复杂性的MAD方法。
AI.method: RUMAD将动态通信拓扑控制建模为强化学习问题。它使用内容无关的观测机制来捕捉辩论动态，并采用多目标奖励来衡量解决方案质量、凝聚力和效率。通过PPO训练的控制器动态调整通信图的边权，并使用双阈值机制控制智能体激活和信息可见性。
AI.result: 在MMLU、GSM8K和GPQA基准测试中，RUMAD将令牌成本降低了80%以上，同时提高了推理准确性。在MMLU上训练的RUMAD在零样本情况下对领域外（OOD）任务表现出强大的泛化能力，表明其学习到的通信策略捕获了任务无关的有效多智能体协调原则。
AI.conclusion: RUMAD是一种高效且鲁棒的多智能体辩论框架，能够有效平衡推理准确性、共识形成和计算效率，适用于具有实际资源限制的多智能体推理应用。

title: RF-Agent: Automated Reward Function Design via Language Agent Tree Search
comment: 39 pages, 9 tables, 11 figures, Project page see https://github.com/deng-ai-lab/RF-Agent
summary: Designing efficient reward functions for low-level control tasks is a challenging problem. Recent research aims to reduce reliance on expert experience by using Large Language Models (LLMs) with task information to generate dense reward functions. These methods typically rely on training results as feedback, iteratively generating new reward functions with greedy or evolutionary algorithms. However, they suffer from poor utilization of historical feedback and inefficient search, resulting in limited improvements in complex control tasks. To address this challenge, we propose RF-Agent, a framework that treats LLMs as language agents and frames reward function design as a sequential decision-making process, enhancing optimization through better contextual reasoning. RF-Agent integrates Monte Carlo Tree Search (MCTS) to manage the reward design and optimization process, leveraging the multi-stage contextual reasoning ability of LLMs. This approach better utilizes historical information and improves search efficiency to identify promising reward functions. Outstanding experimental results in 17 diverse low-level control tasks demonstrate the effectiveness of our method. The source code is available at https://github.com/deng-ai-lab/RF-Agent.

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为 RF-Agent 的新框架，利用 LLM 作为语言代理，通过 MCTS 将奖励函数设计视为一个序列决策问题，以提高低级控制任务的奖励函数设计效率和效果。
AI.motivation: 现有使用 LLM 生成奖励函数的方法，虽然减少了对专家经验的依赖，但存在历史反馈利用率低、搜索效率低下等问题，尤其在复杂控制任务中效果有限。
AI.method: RF-Agent 将 LLM 视为语言代理，将奖励函数设计建模为序列决策过程。它集成了蒙特卡洛树搜索 (MCTS) 来管理奖励设计和优化过程，利用 LLM 的多阶段上下文推理能力来提升优化效果。
AI.result: RF-Agent 在 17 个不同的低级控制任务中取得了优异的实验结果，证明了其方法的有效性。
AI.conclusion: RF-Agent 框架通过将奖励函数设计视为一个由 LLM 和 MCTS 驱动的序列决策过程，能够更有效地利用历史信息和提高搜索效率，从而在复杂低级控制任务中设计出更优的奖励函数。

title: OmniTrack: General Motion Tracking via Physics-Consistent Reference
summary: Learning motion tracking from rich human motion data is a foundational task for achieving general control in humanoid robots, enabling them to perform diverse behaviors. However, discrepancies in morphology and dynamics between humans and robots, combined with data noise, introduce physically infeasible artifacts in reference motions, such as floating and penetration. During both training and execution, these artifacts create a conflict between following inaccurate reference motions and maintaining the robot's stability, hindering the development of a generalizable motion tracking policy. To address these challenges, we introduce OmniTrack, a general tracking framework that explicitly decouples physical feasibility from general motion tracking. In the first stage, a privileged generalist policy generates physically plausible motions that strictly adhere to the robot's dynamics via trajectory rollout in simulation. In the second stage, the general control policy is trained to track these physically feasible motions, ensuring stable and coherent control transfer to the real robot. Experiments show that OmniTrack improves tracking accuracy and demonstrates strong generalization to unseen motions. In real-world tests, OmniTrack achieves hour-long, consistent, and stable tracking, including complex acrobatic motions such as flips and cartwheels. Additionally, we show that OmniTrack supports human-style stable and dynamic online teleoperation, highlighting its robustness and adaptability to varying user inputs.

AI.tldr: OmniTrack 是一个通用运动追踪框架，通过将物理可行性与运动追踪任务分离来解决机器人运动追踪中的物理不可能性问题，从而实现更准确、更稳定的机器人运动控制。
AI.motivation: 人类运动数据中存在的形态和动力学差异、数据噪声会导致参考运动中出现物理上不可行的伪影（如漂浮和穿透），这在训练和执行过程中会与机器人稳定性产生冲突，阻碍了可泛化的运动追踪策略的开发。
AI.method: OmniTrack 分两阶段进行：1. 采用特权通用策略生成物理上可行的运动，通过轨迹滚出来严格遵守机器人动力学。2. 训练通用控制策略来追踪这些物理上可行的运动。
AI.result: OmniTrack 提高了追踪精度，并能很好地泛化到未见过的运动。在真实世界测试中，OmniTrack 实现了长达一小时的稳定追踪，包括翻滚和侧手翻等复杂的杂技动作，并支持人式稳定动态的在线遥操作。
AI.conclusion: OmniTrack 框架通过显式解耦物理可行性与运动追踪，有效地解决了参考运动中的物理不可能性问题，显著提升了机器人的运动追踪性能和泛化能力，并在现实场景中展现了鲁棒性和适应性。

title: GLUScope: A Tool for Analyzing GLU Neurons in Transformer Language Models
comment: 6 pages for main body, 9 pages in total. 4 figures
summary: We present GLUScope, an open-source tool for analyzing neurons in Transformer-based language models, intended for interpretability researchers. We focus on more recent models than previous tools do; specifically we consider gated activation functions such as SwiGLU. This introduces a new challenge: understanding positive activations is not enough. Instead, both the gate and the in activation of a neuron can be positive or negative, leading to four different possible sign combinations that in some cases have quite different functionalities. Accordingly, for any neuron, our tool shows text examples for each of the four sign combinations, and indicates how often each combination occurs. We describe examples of how our tool can lead to novel insights. A demo is available at https: //sjgerstner.github.io/gluscope.

AI.tldr: GLUScope 是一个开源工具，用于分析 Transformer 语言模型中的神经元，特别关注包含 SwiGLU 等门控激活函数的模型，通过展示四种可能的激活符号组合来提供更深入的解释性分析。
AI.motivation: 现有的神经元分析工具未能充分支持包含门控激活函数（如 SwiGLU）的现代 Transformer 模型，而这些模型的解释性分析需要同时考虑正负激活值及其组合。
AI.method: 开发了一个名为 GLUScope 的开源工具，该工具能够分析 Transformer 模型中的神经元，并为每种神经元展示四种可能的激活符号组合（正/负，门/激活）的文本示例，同时统计它们出现的频率。
AI.result: GLUScope 能够生成每种激活符号组合的文本示例，并指示其发生频率，从而为解释性研究提供新的视角和可能性。
AI.conclusion: GLUScope 提供了一种有效的方法来理解包含门控激活函数的 Transformer 模型中的神经元功能，通过展示不同激活符号组合的示例，有望促进新的解释性发现。

title: Pessimistic Auxiliary Policy for Offline Reinforcement Learning
summary: Offline reinforcement learning aims to learn an agent from pre-collected datasets, avoiding unsafe and inefficient real-time interaction. However, inevitable access to out-ofdistribution actions during the learning process introduces approximation errors, causing the error accumulation and considerable overestimation. In this paper, we construct a new pessimistic auxiliary policy for sampling reliable actions. Specifically, we develop a pessimistic auxiliary strategy by maximizing the lower confidence bound of the Q-function. The pessimistic auxiliary strategy exhibits a relatively high value and low uncertainty in the vicinity of the learned policy, avoiding the learned policy sampling high-value actions with potentially high errors during the learning process. Less approximation error introduced by sampled action from pessimistic auxiliary strategy leads to the alleviation of error accumulation. Extensive experiments on offline reinforcement learning benchmarks reveal that utilizing the pessimistic auxiliary strategy can effectively improve the efficacy of other offline RL approaches.

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种悲观辅助策略，通过最大化Q函数置信区间的下界来选择可靠的动作，以减少离线强化学习中的近似误差和过估计问题，并在基准测试中证明了其有效性。
AI.motivation: 离线强化学习在利用已有数据集学习时，会面临由分布外动作引起的近似误差，进而导致误差累积和过高估计。这促使研究者寻求减少这些误差的方法。
AI.method: 本文构建了一种新的悲观辅助策略，通过最大化Q函数置信区间的下界来选择动作。该策略旨在选择在学习策略附近具有高价值且低不确定性的动作，从而避免学习策略采样到高价值但可能存在大误差的动作。
AI.result: 通过采用悲观辅助策略，可以减少采样动作带来的近似误差，从而缓解误差累积。在离线强化学习的基准测试中，实验表明该策略能有效提升其他离线强化学习方法的性能。
AI.conclusion: 所提出的悲观辅助策略通过选择可靠的动作，能够有效减少离线强化学习中的近似误差和误差累积，并增强现有离线强化学习算法的效能。

title: OmniXtreme: Breaking the Generality Barrier in High-Dynamic Humanoid Control
summary: High-fidelity motion tracking serves as the ultimate litmus test for generalizable, human-level motor skills. However, current policies often hit a "generality barrier": as motion libraries scale in diversity, tracking fidelity inevitably collapses - especially for real-world deployment of high-dynamic motions. We identify this failure as the result of two compounding factors: the learning bottleneck in scaling multi-motion optimization and the physical executability constraints that arise in real-world actuation. To overcome these challenges, we introduce OmniXtreme, a scalable framework that decouples general motor skill learning from sim-to-real physical skill refinement. Our approach uses a flow-matching policy with high-capacity architectures to scale representation capacity without interference-intensive multi-motion RL optimization, followed by an actuation-aware refinement phase that ensures robust performance on physical hardware. Extensive experiments demonstrate that OmniXtreme maintains high-fidelity tracking across diverse, high-difficulty datasets. On real robots, the unified policy successfully executes multiple extreme motions, effectively breaking the long-standing fidelity-scalability trade-off in high-dynamic humanoid control.

AI.tldr: OmniXtreme 框架通过解耦动作学习与物理精炼，解决了现有运动追踪技术在处理多样化和高难度动作时保真度下降的问题，并在真实机器人上实现了高保真度的通用极端动作控制。
AI.motivation: 现有运动追踪策略在处理多样化动作库时保真度会下降，尤其是在高动态真实世界应用中，这归因于多动作优化中的学习瓶颈和实际执行中的物理约束。
AI.method: OmniXtreme 框架包含两个阶段：1. 使用具有高容量架构的流匹配策略进行大规模的运动技能学习，避免了多动作强化学习的优化干扰；2. 通过一个考虑执行器约束的精炼阶段，确保在物理硬件上的鲁棒性。
AI.result: OmniXtreme 在处理多样化、高难度数据集时保持了高保真度的运动追踪。在真实机器人上，该框架成功执行了多个极端动作。
AI.conclusion: OmniXtreme 框架有效打破了高动态人形机器人控制中保真度与可扩展性之间的权衡，实现了跨多样化和高难度动作的高保真度追踪。

title: ProtoDCS: Towards Robust and Efficient Open-Set Test-Time Adaptation for Vision-Language Models
summary: Large-scale Vision-Language Models (VLMs) exhibit strong zero-shot recognition, yet their real-world deployment is challenged by distribution shifts. While Test-Time Adaptation (TTA) can mitigate this, existing VLM-based TTA methods operate under a closed-set assumption, failing in open-set scenarios where test streams contain both covariate-shifted in-distribution (csID) and out-of-distribution (csOOD) data. This leads to a critical difficulty: the model must discriminate unknown csOOD samples to avoid interference while simultaneously adapting to known csID classes for accuracy. Current open-set TTA (OSTTA) methods rely on hard thresholds for separation and entropy minimization for adaptation. These strategies are brittle, often misclassifying ambiguous csOOD samples and inducing overconfident predictions, and their parameter-update mechanism is computationally prohibitive for VLMs. To address these limitations, we propose Prototype-based Double-Check Separation (ProtoDCS), a robust framework for OSTTA that effectively separates csID and csOOD samples, enabling safe and efficient adaptation of VLMs to csID data. Our main contributions are: (1) a novel double-check separation mechanism employing probabilistic Gaussian Mixture Model (GMM) verification to replace brittle thresholding; and (2) an evidence-driven adaptation strategy utilizing uncertainty-aware loss and efficient prototype-level updates, mitigating overconfidence and reducing computational overhead. Extensive experiments on CIFAR-10/100-C and Tiny-ImageNet-C demonstrate that ProtoDCS achieves state-of-the-art performance, significantly boosting both known-class accuracy and OOD detection metrics. Code will be available at https://github.com/O-YangF/ProtoDCS.

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为ProtoDCS的框架，用于解决大规模视觉语言模型（VLMs）在开放集测试时（即测试数据包含已知类别和未知类别）的分布偏移问题。ProtoDCS通过高斯混合模型（GMM）进行双重检查来分离已知和未知样本，并采用基于不确定性的损失函数和原型级别更新来安全有效地适应已知类别数据。
AI.motivation: 现有的大规模视觉语言模型（VLMs）在实际部署中面临分布偏移的挑战。虽然测试时自适应（TTA）可以缓解这个问题，但现有的VLM-TTA方法在开放集场景下表现不佳，即测试数据可能包含分布内（csID）和分布外（csOOD）数据。在这种情况下，模型需要区分未知的csOOD样本以避免干扰，同时适应已知的csID类别以提高准确性。现有的开放集TTA（OSTTA）方法依赖于硬阈值和熵最小化，这两种策略都存在脆弱性，容易导致误分类和过度自信，且其参数更新机制对VLMs来说计算成本过高。
AI.method: ProtoDCS框架包含两个核心创新：（1）一种新颖的双重检查分离机制，使用概率性的高斯混合模型（GMM）验证来替代脆弱的阈值分割方法。（2）一种证据驱动的自适应策略，利用不确定性感知的损失函数和高效的原型级别更新，以减轻模型的过度自信并降低计算开销。
AI.result: 在CIFAR-10/100-C和Tiny-ImageNet-C数据集上的大量实验表明，ProtoDCS实现了最先进的性能，显著提高了已知类别的准确率和分布外（OOD）检测指标。
AI.conclusion: ProtoDCS是一个鲁棒的开放集测试时自适应（OSTTA）框架，能够有效地区分csID和csOOD样本，并安全高效地使VLMs适应csID数据，克服了现有方法的局限性。

title: CLFEC: A New Task for Unified Linguistic and Factual Error Correction in paragraph-level Chinese Professional Writing
summary: Chinese text correction has traditionally focused on spelling and grammar, while factual error correction is usually treated separately. However, in paragraph-level Chinese professional writing, linguistic (word/grammar/punctuation) and factual errors frequently co-occur and interact, making unified correction both necessary and challenging. This paper introduces CLFEC (Chinese Linguistic & Factual Error Correction), a new task for joint linguistic and factual correction. We construct a mixed, multi-domain Chinese professional writing dataset spanning current affairs, finance, law, and medicine. We then conduct a systematic study of LLM-based correction paradigms, from prompting to retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) and agentic workflows. The analysis reveals practical challenges, including limited generalization of specialized correction models, the need for evidence grounding for factual repair, the difficulty of mixed-error paragraphs, and over-correction on clean inputs. Results further show that handling linguistic and factual Error within the same context outperform decoupled processes, and that agentic workflows can be effective with suitable backbone models. Overall, our dataset and empirical findings provide guidance for building reliable, fully automatic proofreading systems in industrial settings.

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一个名为CLFEC的新任务，旨在统一纠正中文专业写作中的语言和事实性错误。研究了基于大语言模型的多种纠错方法，并发现联合纠错和基于Agent的工作流在处理混合错误时表现更优，同时指出了当前模型在泛化性、证据溯源和避免过度纠错方面面临的挑战。
AI.motivation: 在中文专业写作中，语言错误（如拼写、语法）和事实性错误经常同时出现并相互影响，因此需要一个统一的纠错方法，但这也带来了挑战。
AI.method: 提出CLFEC（Chinese Linguistic & Factual Error Correction）任务；构建了一个包含时事、金融、法律和医学等多领域中文专业写作的混合数据集；系统研究了基于大语言模型的纠错范式，包括提示（prompting）、检索增强生成（RAG）和Agent工作流。
AI.result: 揭示了模型泛化能力有限、事实纠错需要证据支持、混合错误段落处理困难以及对干净输入过度纠错等实际挑战。研究表明，在同一上下文中处理语言和事实性错误优于解耦处理，且合适的Agent工作流能有效纠错。
AI.conclusion: 本文提出的CLFEC任务、数据集和实证研究为构建可靠的、全自动的工业界中文校对系统提供了指导。

title: Suppressing Prior-Comparison Hallucinations in Radiology Report Generation via Semantically Decoupled Latent Steering
summary: Automated radiology report generation using vision-language models (VLMs) is limited by the risk of prior-comparison hallucination, where the model generates historical findings unsupported by the current study. We address this challenge with a training-free, inference-time control framework termed Semantically Decoupled Latent Steering (SDLS). Unlike generic activation steering, which often suffers from semantic entanglement, our approach constructs a semantic-free intervention vector via large language model (LLM)-driven semantic decomposition followed by $QR$-based orthogonalization. This orthogonalization step is critical. It leverages geometric constraints to filter out the clinical semantics often entangled in standard principal component analysis (PCA) directions, ensuring that the steering vector targets only the ``historical comparison" axis. We validate our method on the BiomedGPT foundation model, demonstrating that it overcomes the trade-off between hallucination suppression and clinical accuracy. Extensive experiments on MIMIC-CXR, and zero-shot transfer evaluation on CheXpert Plus and IU-Xray, demonstrate the robustness of our approach. Quantitative evaluations on MIMIC-CXR show that our approach significantly reduces the probability of historical hallucinations (FilBERT score decreases from 0.2373 to 0.1889) and improves clinical label fidelity (CheXpert macro-F1 increases from 0.2242 to 0.3208). Supplementary evaluations confirm that the structural integrity of the clinical narrative is maintained.

AI.tldr: 研究提出了一种名为SDLS的训练免费、推理时控制框架，用于解决自动化放射报告生成中由历史比较幻觉引起的问题，通过LLM驱动的语义分解和QR正交化来构建一个独立的干预向量，成功减少了历史幻觉并提高了临床准确性。
AI.motivation: 自动化放射报告生成过程中，视觉语言模型（VLMs）容易产生与当前研究不符的历史比较幻觉，影响报告的准确性。
AI.method: 提出Semantically Decoupled Latent Steering (SDLS)框架。该框架通过大型语言模型（LLM）驱动的语义分解，然后进行QR正交化，构建一个独立的、不受临床语义影响的干预向量，用于在推理时控制模型的生成过程，使其专注于“历史比较”这一维度。
AI.result: 在BiomedGPT模型上进行了验证，SDLS框架成功克服了抑制幻觉和保持临床准确性之间的权衡。在MIMIC-CXR数据集上，历史幻觉的概率显著降低（FilBERT分数从0.2373降至0.1889），临床标签保真度有所提高（CheXpert宏F1分数从0.2242升至0.3208）。在CheXpert Plus和IU-Xray数据集上的零样本迁移评估也显示了方法的鲁棒性。
AI.conclusion: SDLS是一种有效的训练免费、推理时控制框架，能够显著减少自动化放射报告生成中的历史比较幻觉，同时保持甚至提高报告的临床准确性和整体叙事结构。

title: Hybrid Offline-Online Reinforcement Learning for Sensorless, High-Precision Force Regulation in Surgical Robotic Grasping
summary: Precise grasp force regulation in tendon-driven surgical instruments is fundamentally limited by nonlinear coupling between motor dynamics, transmission compliance, friction, and distal mechanics. Existing solutions typically rely on distal force sensing or analytical compensation, increasing hardware complexity or degrading performance under dynamic motion. We present a sensorless control framework that combines physics-consistent modeling and hybrid reinforcement learning to achieve high-precision distal force regulation in a proximally actuated surgical end-effector. We develop a first-principles digital twin of the da Vinci Xi grasping mechanism that captures coupled electrical, transmission, and jaw dynamics within a unified differential-algebraic formulation. To safely learn control policies in this stiff and highly nonlinear system, we introduce a three-stage pipeline:(i)a receding-horizon CMA-ES oracle that generates dynamically feasible expert trajectories,(ii)fully offline policy learning via Implicit Q-Learning to ensure stable initialization without unsafe exploration, and (iii)online refinement using TD3 for adaptation to on-policy dynamics. The resulting policy directly maps proximal measurements to motor voltages and requires no distal sensing. In simulation, the controller maintains grasp force within 1% of the desired reference during multi-harmonic jaw motion. Hardware experiments demonstrate average force errors below 4% across diverse trajectories, validating sim-to-real transfer. The learned policy contains approximately 71k param and executes at kH rates, enabling real-time deployment. These results demonstrate that high-fidelity modeling combined with structured offline-online RL can recover precise distal force behavior without additional sensing, offering a scalable and mechanically compatible solution for surgical robotic manipulation.

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于物理模型和混合强化学习的无传感器控制框架，用于精确调节近端驱动手术器械的末端执行器抓取力。
AI.motivation: 现有手术器械在精确控制抓取力方面存在局限，通常需要末端力传感或复杂的分析补偿，这会增加硬件复杂性或影响动态性能。本研究旨在开发一种无需末端力传感的控制方案。
AI.method: 研究人员首先构建了一个达芬奇手术机器人抓取机构的数字孪生模型，该模型采用微分代数方程形式统一了电机、传动和夹爪的耦合动力学。然后，他们设计了一个三阶段的强化学习训练流程：1. 使用CMA-ES生成专家轨迹；2. 离线使用Implicit Q-Learning学习策略；3. 在线使用TD3进行策略精炼。最终的策略直接将近端测量映射到电机电压。
AI.result: 在仿真中，该控制器在多谐波运动下能将抓取力误差控制在目标值的1%以内。在实际硬件实验中，在各种轨迹下平均力误差低于4%，验证了仿真到实际的迁移能力。学习到的策略参数量为71k，执行速度可达kHz。
AI.conclusion: 结合高保真建模和结构化的离线-在线强化学习，可以在无需额外传感的情况下实现精确的末端力控制，为手术机器人操控提供了一种可扩展且机械兼容的解决方案。

title: The Astonishing Ability of Large Language Models to Parse Jabberwockified Language
comment: Submitted to the 2026 Annual Meeting of the Cognitive Science Society
summary: We show that large language models (LLMs) have an astonishing ability to recover meaning from severely degraded English texts. Texts in which content words have been randomly substituted by nonsense strings, e.g., "At the ghybe of the swuint, we are haiveed to Wourge Phrear-gwurr, who sproles into an ghitch flount with his crurp", can be translated to conventional English that is, in many cases, close to the original text, e.g., "At the start of the story, we meet a man, Chow, who moves into an apartment building with his wife." These results show that structural cues (e.g., morphosyntax, closed-class words) constrain lexical meaning to a much larger degree than imagined. Although the abilities of LLMs to make sense of "Jabberwockified" English are clearly superhuman, they are highly relevant to understanding linguistic structure and suggest that efficient language processing either in biological or artificial systems likely benefits from very tight integration between syntax, lexical semantics, and general world knowledge.

AI.tldr: 大型语言模型 (LLMs) 能够从严重损坏的英文文本中恢复大量含义，即使在内容词被随机替换成无意义字符串的情况下也能做到。这表明语言结构（如词法、闭合类词）对词汇意义的约束作用比之前想象的要大得多。LLMs 的这种能力是超人的，但对于理解语言结构非常重要，并暗示高效的语言处理（无论是生物还是人工系统）都受益于句法、词汇语义和世界知识的紧密结合。
AI.motivation: 探索大型语言模型（LLMs）在处理被严重破坏的英文文本（例如，内容词被替换成无意义字符串）时理解和恢复文本含义的能力。
AI.method: 通过给 LLMs 提供被“Jabberwockified”的（即内容词被替换成无意义字符串的）英文文本，并观察其能否将其翻译回接近原文的正常英文。
AI.result: LLMs 展现出了惊人的能力，能够从被严重破坏的文本中恢复出接近原文的含义。这表明语言的结构性线索（如词法、闭合类词）对词汇意义的约束作用非常强大。
AI.conclusion: LLMs 对“Jabberwockified”英文的理解能力远超人类，这有力地证明了语言结构对语义的强大约束力。研究结果暗示，无论是生物还是人工系统，高效的语言处理都依赖于句法、词汇语义和世界知识的深度整合。

title: Portfolio Reinforcement Learning with Scenario-Context Rollout
summary:
Market regime shifts induce distribution shifts that can degrade the performance of portfolio rebalancing policies. We propose macro-conditioned scenario-context rollout (SCR) that generates plausible next-day multivariate return scenarios under stress events. However, doing so faces new challenges, as history will never tell what would have happened differently. As a result, incorporating scenario-based rewards from rollouts introduces a reward--transition mismatch in temporal-difference learning, destabilizing RL critic training.
  We analyze this inconsistency and show it leads to a mixed evaluation target. Guided by this analysis, we construct a counterfactual next state using the rollout-implied continuations and augment the critic agent's bootstrap target. Doing so stabilizes the learning and provides a viable bias-variance tradeoff.
  In out-of-sample evaluations across 31 distinct universes of U.S. equity and ETF portfolios, our method improves Sharpe ratio by up to 76% and reduces maximum drawdown by up to 53% compared with classic and RL-based portfolio rebalancing baselines.

AI.tldr: 该研究提出了一种名为宏观条件场景-上下文回放 (SCR) 的方法，用于生成压力事件下的多变量收益场景，以解决市场状态转移对投资组合再平衡策略性能的影响。通过分析并修正时间差学习中的奖励-转移不匹配问题，该方法在实际投资组合评估中显著提高了夏普比率并降低了最大回撤。
AI.motivation: 市场状态的转变会导致数据分布变化，从而降低投资组合再平衡策略的性能。现有的方法在处理压力事件下的市场情景生成时面临挑战，特别是在深度强化学习中会导致奖励-转移不匹配，影响 Critic 学习的稳定性。
AI.method: 研究提出宏观条件场景-上下文回放 (SCR) 方法来生成压力事件下的收益场景。通过分析时间差学习中的奖励-转移不匹配问题，并构建反事实的下一状态来增强 Critic 的引导目标，以稳定学习过程并实现偏差-方差的权衡。
AI.result: 在 31 个不同的美国股票和 ETF 投资组合上的样本外评估显示，与经典的基于强化学习的投资组合再平衡基线相比，该方法将夏普比率提高了高达 76%，并将最大回撤降低了高达 53%。
AI.conclusion: 所提出的宏观条件场景-上下文回放 (SCR) 方法及其对 Critic 学习目标的修正，能够有效解决市场状态转移带来的挑战，稳定强化学习过程，并在实际投资组合管理中提供显著的性能提升。

title: Benchmarking BERT-based Models for Sentence-level Topic Classification in Nepali Language
comment: 5 pages, 2 figures. Accepted and presented at the Regional International Conference on Natural Language Processing (RegICON 2025), Gauhati University, Guwahati, India, November 27-29, 2025. To appear in the conference proceedings. Accepted papers list available at: https://www.regicon2025.in/accepted-papers
summary: Transformer-based models such as BERT have significantly advanced Natural Language Processing (NLP) across many languages. However, Nepali, a low-resource language written in Devanagari script, remains relatively underexplored. This study benchmarks multilingual, Indic, Hindi, and Nepali BERT variants to evaluate their effectiveness in Nepali topic classification. Ten pre-trained models, including mBERT, XLM-R, MuRIL, DevBERT, HindiBERT, IndicBERT, and NepBERTa, were fine-tuned and tested on the balanced Nepali dataset containing 25,006 sentences across five conceptual domains and the performance was evaluated using accuracy, weighted precision, recall, F1-score, and AUROC metrics. The results reveal that Indic models, particularly MuRIL-large, achieved the highest F1-score of 90.60%, outperforming multilingual and monolingual models. NepBERTa also performed competitively with an F1-score of 88.26%. Overall, these findings establish a robust baseline for future document-level classification and broader Nepali NLP applications.

AI.tldr: 该研究评估了多种预训练的 Transformer 模型在尼泊尔语主题分类任务上的表现，发现 Indic 模型（特别是 MuRIL-large）表现最佳。
AI.motivation: 尽管 Transformer 模型在 NLP 领域取得了显著进展，但对尼泊尔语等低资源语言的研究相对较少。本研究旨在评估现有模型在尼泊尔语主题分类任务上的有效性。
AI.method: 研究对十种预训练模型（包括 mBERT, XLM-R, MuRIL, DevBERT, HindiBERT, IndicBERT, NepBERTa）进行了微调，并在包含 25,006 句、涵盖五个主题领域的平衡尼泊尔语数据集上进行了测试。评估指标包括准确率、加权精确率、召回率、F1 分数和 AUROC。
AI.result: Indic 模型，尤其是 MuRIL-large，取得了最高的 F1 分数（90.60%），优于多语言和单语言模型。NepBERTa 的 F1 分数为 88.26%，表现也具有竞争力。
AI.conclusion: 该研究为尼泊尔语文档级分类和更广泛的 NLP 应用奠定了坚实的基础，并表明 Indic 模型在尼泊尔语任务上具有优势。

title: TSC: Topology-Conditioned Stackelberg Coordination for Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning in Interactive Driving
summary: Safe and efficient autonomous driving in dense traffic is fundamentally a decentralized multi-agent coordination problem, where interactions at conflict points such as merging and weaving must be resolved reliably under partial observability. With only local and incomplete cues, interaction patterns can change rapidly, often causing unstable behaviors such as oscillatory yielding or unsafe commitments. Existing multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL) approaches either adopt synchronous decision-making, which exacerbate non-stationarity, or depend on centralized sequencing mechanisms that scale poorly as traffic density increases. To address these limitations, we propose Topology-conditioned Stackelberg Coordination (TSC), a learning framework for decentralized interactive driving under communication-free execution, which extracts a time-varying directed priority graph from braid-inspired weaving relations between trajectories, thereby defining local leader-follower dependencies without constructing a global order of play. Conditioned on this graph, TSC endogenously factorizes dense interactions into graph-local Stackelberg subgames and, under centralized training and decentralized execution (CTDE), learns a sequential coordination policy that anticipates leaders via action prediction and trains followers through action-conditioned value learning to approximate local best responses, improving training stability and safety in dense traffic. Experiments across four dense traffic scenarios show that TSC achieves superior performance over representative MARL baselines across key metrics, most notably reducing collisions while maintaining competitive traffic efficiency and control smoothness.

AI.tldr: 提出了一种名为拓扑条件Stackelberg协调（TSC）的学习框架，用于在通信受限的情况下实现去中心化、交互式的自动驾驶。TSC通过提取动态优先图来建立局部的领导-跟随者依赖关系，并将复杂交互分解为局部的Stackelberg子博弈，从而提高训练稳定性和安全性。
AI.motivation: 现有的多智能体强化学习（MARL）方法在处理稠密交通中的交互式驾驶时存在不足，如同步决策导致非平稳性，或集中式排序机制扩展性差。在部分可观测且交互模式快速变化的情况下，需要一种更有效的协调机制。
AI.method: TSC框架提取一个随时间变化的定向优先图，该图基于轨迹间的编织关系，定义了局部的领导-跟随者依赖。在此基础上，TSC将交互分解为图局部的Stackelberg子博弈。在集中训练和去中心化执行（CTDE）的范式下，TSC学习一个序列协调策略，通过动作预测来预测领导者，并通过动作条件价值学习来训练跟随者，以近似局部最优响应。
AI.result: TSC在四个稠密交通场景的实验中，相比代表性的MARL基线，在关键指标上均表现更优，尤其是在减少碰撞的同时保持了良好的交通效率和控制平滑度。
AI.conclusion: TSC框架能够有效地解决去中心化交互式驾驶中的非平稳性和可扩展性问题，在稠密交通场景下，通过学习局部的领导-跟随者关系和Stackelberg子博弈，能够实现更安全、更高效的协调。

title: CIRCLE: A Framework for Evaluating AI from a Real-World Lens
summary: This paper proposes CIRCLE, a six-stage, lifecycle-based framework to bridge the reality gap between model-centric performance metrics and AI's materialized outcomes in deployment. While existing frameworks like MLOps focus on system stability and benchmarks measure abstract capabilities, decision-makers outside the AI stack lack systematic evidence about the behavior of AI technologies under real-world user variability and constraints. CIRCLE operationalizes the Validation phase of TEVV (Test, Evaluation, Verification, and Validation) by formalizing the translation of stakeholder concerns outside the stack into measurable signals. Unlike participatory design, which often remains localized, or algorithmic audits, which are often retrospective, CIRCLE provides a structured, prospective protocol for linking context-sensitive qualitative insights to scalable quantitative metrics. By integrating methods such as field testing, red teaming, and longitudinal studies into a coordinated pipeline, CIRCLE produces systematic knowledge: evidence that is comparable across sites yet sensitive to local context. This can enable governance based on materialized downstream effects rather than theoretical capabilities.

AI.tldr: 本文提出了CIRCLE框架，一个基于生命周期的六阶段模型，旨在弥合模型性能指标与AI实际部署效果之间的差距，为AI治理提供基于实际下游影响的证据。
AI.motivation: 现有的MLOps和基准测试框架未能提供AI在真实用户变化和约束下行为的系统性证据，尤其对AI堆栈之外的决策者而言。这导致AI治理更多地基于理论能力而非实际效果。
AI.method: CIRCLE框架通过正式化将AI堆栈之外的利益相关者关切转化为可衡量信号，从而操作化TEVV（测试、评估、验证、确认）中的确认阶段。它整合了现场测试、红队演练和纵向研究等方法，形成一个协调的流程，产生可比且对本地情境敏感的系统化知识。
AI.result: CIRCLE框架能够将定性的、情境敏感的见解与可扩展的定量指标联系起来，产生可跨站点比较但又能反映本地特异性的系统化证据。这使得AI治理可以基于AI的实际下游影响。
AI.conclusion: CIRCLE提供了一个结构化、前瞻性的协议，用于将AI的实际部署效果与模型性能指标联系起来，为AI治理提供了新的方法，使其能够基于AI的实际下游影响，而非仅理论能力进行决策。

title: Vision-Language Semantic Grounding for Multi-Domain Crop-Weed Segmentation
summary: Fine-grained crop-weed segmentation is essential for enabling targeted herbicide application in precision agriculture. However, existing deep learning models struggle to generalize across heterogeneous agricultural environments due to reliance on dataset-specific visual features. We propose Vision-Language Weed Segmentation (VL-WS), a novel framework that addresses this limitation by grounding pixel-level segmentation in semantically aligned, domain-invariant representations. Our architecture employs a dual-encoder design, where frozen Contrastive Language-Image Pretraining (CLIP) embeddings and task-specific spatial features are fused and modulated via Feature-wise Linear Modulation (FiLM) layers conditioned on natural language captions. This design enables image level textual descriptions to guide channel-wise feature refinement while preserving fine-grained spatial localization. Unlike prior works restricted to training and evaluation on single-source datasets, VL-WS is trained on a unified corpus that includes close-range ground imagery (robotic platforms) and high-altitude UAV imagery, covering diverse crop types, weed species, growth stages, and sensing conditions. Experimental results across four benchmark datasets demonstrate the effectiveness of our framework, with VL-WS achieving a mean Dice score of 91.64% and outperforming the CNN baseline by 4.98%. The largest gains occur on the most challenging weed class, where VL-WS attains 80.45% Dice score compared to 65.03% for the best baseline, representing a 15.42% improvement. VL-WS further maintains stable weed segmentation performance under limited target-domain supervision, indicating improved generalization and data efficiency. These findings highlight the potential of vision-language alignment to enable scalable, label-efficient segmentation models deployable across diverse real-world agricultural domains.

AI.tldr: 提出了一种名为VL-WS的视觉-语言杂草分割新框架，通过利用CLIP嵌入和文本描述来指导特征细化，实现了跨不同农业环境的泛化能力，并在多项基准测试中取得了显著的性能提升，尤其是在最具挑战性的杂草类别上。
AI.motivation: 现有深度学习模型在精准农业中因依赖数据集特定的视觉特征而难以泛化到异构的农业环境。因此，需要一种能够基于语义对齐的、领域不变的表示来指导像素级分割的模型。
AI.method: 提出了一种名为VL-WS的框架，采用双编码器设计。该框架融合了冻结的CLIP嵌入和特定任务的空间特征，并通过条件于自然语言字幕的FiLM层进行调制。这种设计允许图像级别的文本描述来指导通道级特征的细化，同时保留精细的空间定位。
AI.result: 在四个基准数据集上，VL-WS取得了91.64%的平均Dice分数，比CNN基线高出4.98%。在最具挑战性的杂草类别上，VL-WS的Dice分数达到了80.45%，而最佳基线为65.03%，提升了15.42%。VL-WS在有限的目标领域监督下也能保持稳定的杂草分割性能，表明其泛化能力和数据效率有所提高。
AI.conclusion: 视觉-语言对齐有潜力实现可扩展、标签效率高的分割模型，能够部署到多样化的真实农业领域。VL-WS通过利用文本描述来改善跨领域泛化，证明了其在精准农业杂草分割中的有效性。

title: ABPolicy: Asynchronous B-Spline Flow Policy for Real-Time and Smooth Robotic Manipulation
summary: Robotic manipulation requires policies that are smooth and responsive to evolving observations. However, synchronous inference in the raw action space introduces several challenges, including intra-chunk jitter, inter-chunk discontinuities, and stop-and-go execution. These issues undermine a policy's smoothness and its responsiveness to environmental changes. We propose ABPolicy, an asynchronous flow-matching policy that operates in a B-spline control-point action space. First, the B-spline representation ensures intra-chunk smoothness. Second, we introduce bidirectional action prediction coupled with refitting optimization to enforce inter-chunk continuity. Finally, by leveraging asynchronous inference, ABPolicy delivers real-time, continuous updates. We evaluate ABPolicy across seven tasks encompassing both static settings and dynamic settings with moving objects. Empirical results indicate that ABPolicy reduces trajectory jerk, leading to smoother motion and improved performance. Project website: https://teee000.github.io/ABPolicy/.

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为ABPolicy的异步流匹配策略，它在B样条控制点动作空间中操作，以解决机器人操作中动作的平滑性和响应性问题。
AI.motivation: 现有的机器人操作策略在原始动作空间中进行同步推理时，存在块内抖动、块间不连续和停止-进行执行等问题，这些问题会影响策略的平滑性和对环境变化的响应能力。
AI.method: ABPolicy利用B样条表示保证块内平滑性，并通过引入双向动作预测和重拟合优化来强制执行块间连续性。同时，它利用异步推理实现实时、连续的更新。
AI.result: 在七个静态和动态任务上的实验表明，ABPolicy降低了轨迹的加加速度，从而实现了更平滑的运动并提高了性能。
AI.conclusion: ABPolicy通过在B样条控制点动作空间中进行异步流匹配，有效解决了机器人操作中动作平滑性和响应性问题，并取得了更好的实验结果。

title: Any Model, Any Place, Any Time: Get Remote Sensing Foundation Model Embeddings On Demand
summary: The remote sensing community is witnessing a rapid growth of foundation models, which provide powerful embeddings for a wide range of downstream tasks. However, practical adoption and fair comparison remain challenging due to substantial heterogeneity in model release formats, platforms and interfaces, and input data specifications. These inconsistencies significantly increase the cost of obtaining, using, and benchmarking embeddings across models. To address this issue, we propose rs-embed, a Python library that offers a unified, region of interst (ROI) centric interface: with a single line of code, users can retrieve embeddings from any supported model for any location and any time range. The library also provides efficient batch processing to enable large-scale embedding generation and evaluation. The code is available at: https://github.com/cybergis/rs-embed

AI.tldr: 本文提出rs-embed，一个Python库，旨在解决遥感领域基础模型应用不一致的问题，提供统一的、以兴趣区域为中心的接口，方便用户快速获取和比较不同模型的嵌入表示。
AI.motivation: 遥感领域基础模型数量快速增长，但模型发布格式、平台接口、输入数据等不一致，导致模型采用和公平比较困难，增加了获取、使用和基准测试嵌入表示的成本。
AI.method: 提出rs-embed，一个Python库，提供统一的、以兴趣区域（ROI）为中心的接口。用户只需一行代码即可获取任意位置、任意时间范围内支持模型的嵌入表示。该库还支持高效的批量处理，以便进行大规模嵌入生成和评估。
AI.result: rs-embed提供了一个简化的接口，大大降低了用户跨模型获取和使用遥感基础模型嵌入表示的门槛，并支持大规模应用。
AI.conclusion: rs-embed提供了一个统一的接口和高效的批量处理能力，能够解决遥感基础模型应用中的不一致性问题，促进这些模型的实际应用和公平比较。

title: Towards Source-Aware Object Swapping with Initial Noise Perturbation
comment: This paper is accepted by CVPR 2026 Findings
summary: Object swapping aims to replace a source object in a scene with a reference object while preserving object fidelity, scene fidelity, and object-scene harmony. Existing methods either require per-object finetuning and slow inference or rely on extra paired data that mostly depict the same object across contexts, forcing models to rely on background cues rather than learning cross-object alignment. We propose SourceSwap, a self-supervised and source-aware framework that learns cross-object alignment. Our key insight is to synthesize high-quality pseudo pairs from any image via a frequency-separated perturbation in the initial-noise space, which alters appearance while preserving pose, coarse shape, and scene layout, requiring no videos, multi-view data, or additional images. We then train a dual U-Net with full-source conditioning and a noise-free reference encoder, enabling direct inter-object alignment, zero-shot inference without per-object finetuning, and lightweight iterative refinement. We further introduce SourceBench, a high-quality benchmark with higher resolution, more categories, and richer interactions. Experiments demonstrate that SourceSwap achieves superior fidelity, stronger scene preservation, and more natural harmony, and it transfers well to edits such as subject-driven refinement and face swapping.

AI.tldr: SourceSwap 是一个自监督、源感知框架，通过合成伪配对数据，学习跨对象对齐，实现了高效、零样本的对象替换，并在 SourceBench 基准上表现出色。
AI.motivation: 现有对象替换方法存在需要对每个对象进行微调、推理速度慢或依赖额外配对数据的缺点，后者迫使模型依赖背景线索而非学习跨对象对齐。
AI.method: 该研究提出 SourceSwap 框架，通过频率分离的扰动在初始噪声空间合成伪配对数据，训练双 U-Net 模型，并引入噪声无关的参考编码器，实现跨对象对齐和零样本推理。同时，创建了 SourceBench 基准。
AI.result: SourceSwap 在保真度、场景保持和和谐性方面优于现有方法，并且能很好地迁移到对象驱动的精炼和换脸等编辑任务。
AI.conclusion: SourceSwap 提供了一种高效、零样本的对象替换解决方案，通过合成伪配对数据有效解决了现有方法的局限性，并在创建的高质量基准上验证了其优越性。

title: Teleoperated Omni-directional Dual Arm Mobile Manipulation Robotic System with Shared Control for Retail Store
comment: This work has been accepted for publication in the Proceedings of the IEEE International Conference on Systems, Man, and Cybernetics (SMC 2024). $©$ IEEE. The final version is available via IEEE Xplore
summary: The swiftly expanding retail sector is increasingly adopting autonomous mobile robots empowered by artificial intelligence and machine learning algorithms to gain an edge in the competitive market. However, these autonomous robots encounter challenges in adapting to the dynamic nature of retail products, often struggling to operate autonomously in novel situations. In this study, we introduce an omni-directional dual-arm mobile robot specifically tailored for use in retail environments. Additionally, we propose a tele-operation method that enables shared control between the robot and a human operator. This approach utilizes a Virtual Reality (VR) motion capture system to capture the operator's commands, which are then transmitted to the robot located remotely in a retail setting. Furthermore, the robot is equipped with heterogeneous grippers on both manipulators, facilitating the handling of a wide range of items. We validate the efficacy of the proposed system through testing in a mockup of retail environment, demonstrating its ability to manipulate various commonly encountered retail items using both single and dual-arm coordinated manipulation techniques.

AI.tldr: 本研究提出了一种用于零售环境的、具备双臂和全向移动能力的自主机器人，并结合了VR远程操控和异构夹持器，以应对零售产品动态性带来的挑战。
AI.motivation: 零售机器人难以适应动态多变的零售产品和新颖场景，自主操作能力受限。
AI.method: 设计了一款全向双臂移动机器人，开发了一种基于VR动作捕捉的共享控制远程操作方法，机器人配备了异构夹持器。
AI.result: 在零售环境模拟中，机器人能够通过单臂或双臂协同操作，成功抓取和处理各种零售商品。
AI.conclusion: 提出的全向双臂零售机器人结合VR共享控制和异构夹持器，能够有效处理零售环境中多样化的物品，提高了机器人在复杂场景下的适应性和操作能力。

title: MemEmo: Evaluating Emotion in Memory Systems of Agents
summary: Memory systems address the challenge of context loss in Large Language Model during prolonged interactions. However, compared to human cognition, the efficacy of these systems in processing emotion-related information remains inconclusive. To address this gap, we propose an emotion-enhanced memory evaluation benchmark to assess the performance of mainstream and state-of-the-art memory systems in handling affective information. We developed the \textbf{H}uman-\textbf{L}ike \textbf{M}emory \textbf{E}motion (\textbf{HLME}) dataset, which evaluates memory systems across three dimensions: emotional information extraction, emotional memory updating, and emotional memory question answering. Experimental results indicate that none of the evaluated systems achieve robust performance across all three tasks. Our findings provide an objective perspective on the current deficiencies of memory systems in processing emotional memories and suggest a new trajectory for future research and system optimization.

AI.tldr: 现有的大语言模型记忆系统在处理情绪相关信息方面存在不足，研究提出了HLME数据集来评估这些系统的表现，并发现了它们在情绪信息提取、更新和问答方面的普遍性缺陷。
AI.motivation: 现有的大语言模型记忆系统在处理长对话中的上下文信息方面有所改进，但其在处理情绪相关信息方面的能力尚不明确，与人类认知相比存在差距。
AI.method: 研究提出了一个名为HLME（Human-Like Memory Emotion）的数据集，用于评估主流和先进的记忆系统在情绪信息处理方面的能力。该数据集包含三个评估维度：情绪信息提取、情绪记忆更新和情绪记忆问答。
AI.result: 实验结果表明，在HLME数据集上，没有一个被评估的记忆系统在所有三个任务上都能达到鲁棒的性能。
AI.conclusion: 研究得出的结论是，当前的记忆系统在处理情绪记忆方面存在明显的不足，为未来研究和系统优化指明了新的方向。

title: HiDrop: Hierarchical Vision Token Reduction in MLLMs via Late Injection, Concave Pyramid Pruning, and Early Exit
summary: The quadratic computational cost of processing vision tokens in Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) hinders their widespread adoption. While progressive vision token pruning offers a promising solution, current methods misinterpret shallow layer functions and use rigid schedules, which fail to unlock the full efficiency potential. To address these issues, we propose HiDrop, a framework that aligns token pruning with the true hierarchical function of MLLM layers. HiDrop features two key innovations: (1) Late Injection, which bypasses passive shallow layers to introduce visual tokens exactly where active fusion begins; and (2) Concave Pyramid Pruning with an Early Exit mechanism to dynamically adjust pruning rates across middle and deep layers. This process is optimized via an inter-layer similarity measure and a differentiable top-k operator. To ensure practical efficiency, HiDrop further incorporates persistent positional encoding, FlashAttention-compatible token selection, and parallel decoupling of vision computation to eliminate hidden overhead associated with dynamic token reduction. Extensive experiments show that HiDrop compresses about 90% visual tokens while matching the original performance and accelerating training by 1.72 times. Our work not only sets a new state-of-the-art for efficient MLLM training and inference but also provides valuable insights into the hierarchical nature of multimodal fusion. The code is released at https://github.com/EIT-NLP/HiDrop.

AI.tldr: 提出了一种名为 HiDrop 的框架，通过改进视觉 token 的剪枝策略，显著降低了多模态大语言模型（MLLMs）的计算成本，同时保持了性能并加快了训练速度。
AI.motivation: 现有 MLLMs 的视觉 token 处理计算成本高昂，阻碍了其广泛应用。现有的 token 剪枝方法未能充分利用浅层网络的特性，且剪枝策略过于僵化，无法达到最佳效率。
AI.method: HiDrop 框架包含两项创新：1. Late Injection：将视觉 token 在主动融合开始的层级引入，绕过浅层；2. Concave Pyramid Pruning with Early Exit：动态调整中间和深层网络的剪枝率，并通过层间相似度度量和可微分 top-k 算子进行优化。此外，还引入了持久化位置编码、FlashAttention 兼容的 token 选择以及视觉计算的并行解耦来提高实际效率。
AI.result: HiDrop 能够压缩约 90% 的视觉 token，同时性能与原始模型相当，并将训练速度提高了 1.72 倍。
AI.conclusion: HiDrop 在高效 MLLM 训练和推理方面达到了新的 SOTA 水平，并为理解多模态融合的层级特性提供了新的见解。

title: Bi-level RL-Heuristic Optimization for Real-world Winter Road Maintenance
summary: Winter road maintenance is critical for ensuring public safety and reducing environmental impacts, yet existing methods struggle to manage large-scale routing problems effectively and mostly reply on human decision. This study presents a novel, scalable bi-level optimization framework, validated on real operational data on UK strategic road networks (M25, M6, A1), including interconnected local road networks in surrounding areas for vehicle traversing, as part of the highway operator's efforts to solve existing planning challenges. At the upper level, a reinforcement learning (RL) agent strategically partitions the road network into manageable clusters and optimally allocates resources from multiple depots. At the lower level, a multi-objective vehicle routing problem (VRP) is solved within each cluster, minimizing the maximum vehicle travel time and total carbon emissions. Unlike existing approaches, our method handles large-scale, real-world networks efficiently, explicitly incorporating vehicle-specific constraints, depot capacities, and road segment requirements. Results demonstrate significant improvements, including balanced workloads, reduced maximum travel times below the targeted two-hour threshold, lower emissions, and substantial cost savings. This study illustrates how advanced AI-driven bi-level optimization can directly enhance operational decision-making in real-world transportation and logistics.

AI.tldr: 本研究提出了一种新颖的可扩展双层优化框架，利用强化学习（RL）将道路网络划分为可管理的集群，并优化资源分配，同时在每个集群内解决多目标车辆路径问题（VRP），以最小化车辆最长行驶时间和总碳排放量。该方法在大规模真实路网数据上进行了验证，并能有效处理车辆特定约束、车库容量和路段要求。
AI.motivation: 现有冬季道路维护方法在处理大规模路径规划问题时效率低下，且过度依赖人工决策。本研究旨在解决大规模、真实世界道路维护中的实际规划挑战。
AI.method: 研究提出了一种双层优化框架。上层利用强化学习（RL）代理对道路网络进行战略性划分（聚类）并优化多仓库的资源分配。下层则针对每个集群内的多目标车辆路径问题（VRP）进行求解，目标是最小化最长车辆行驶时间和总碳排放量。该方法考虑了车辆特定约束、仓库容量和路段要求。
AI.result: 实验结果表明，该框架能实现工作负载均衡，将最长车辆行驶时间降至目标的两小时阈值以下，减少碳排放，并带来显著的成本节约。该方法能高效处理大规模、真实世界的路网。
AI.conclusion: 先进的人工智能驱动的双层优化方法能够直接提升真实世界交通和物流运营决策的效率，有效解决冬季道路维护中的路径规划问题。

title: Artificial Agency Program: Curiosity, compression, and communication in agents
comment: This is a working draft. Feedback and criticism is most welcome
summary: This paper presents the Artificial Agency Program (AAP), a position and research agenda for building AI systems as reality embedded, resource-bounded agents whose development is driven by curiosity-as-learning-progress under physical and computational constraints. The central thesis is that AI is most useful when treated as part of an extended human--tool system that increases sensing, understanding, and actuation capability while reducing friction at the interface between people, tools, and environments. The agenda unifies predictive compression, intrinsic motivation, empowerment and control, interface quality (unification), and language/self-communication as selective information bottlenecks. We formulate these ideas as a falsifiable program with explicit costs, staged experiments, and a concrete multimodal tokenized testbed in which an agent allocates limited budget among observation, action, and deliberation. The aim is to provide a conceptual and experimental framework that connects intrinsic motivation, information theory, thermodynamics, bounded rationality, and modern reasoning systems

AI.tldr: 本文提出了人工智能代理项目（AAP），一个关于构建嵌入现实、资源受限的人工智能代理的立场和研究议程。该议程强调利用好奇心驱动学习，并在物理和计算约束下进行开发，将人工智能视为增强人类能力、减少人机交互摩擦的工具。文章将预测压缩、内在动机、赋权与控制、接口质量和语言/自我沟通统一为信息瓶颈。
AI.motivation: 作者认为，人工智能最有用的方式是作为人类-工具系统的延伸，增强感知、理解和驱动能力，同时减少人、工具和环境之间交互的摩擦。他们希望通过一个统一的概念和实验框架来连接内在动机、信息论、热力学、有限理性和现代推理系统。
AI.method: 该研究提出了一种可证伪的项目，包含明确的成本、分阶段的实验，以及一个具体的、多模态的、标记化的测试平台。在这个测试平台中，代理将在有限的预算内分配于观察、行动和审议。文章将预测压缩、内在动机、赋权与控制、接口质量和语言/自我沟通统一为选择性信息瓶颈。
AI.result: 文章提出了人工智能代理项目（AAP），一个研究和开发人工智能代理的框架。该框架关注代理在资源受限环境下的学习和决策过程，并将其与人类工具系统的交互联系起来。
AI.conclusion: 人工智能代理项目（AAP）提供了一个概念和实验框架，旨在连接内在动机、信息论、热力学、有限理性和现代推理系统，以构建更实用、更嵌入现实的人工智能系统。

title: The GRADIEND Python Package: An End-to-End System for Gradient-Based Feature Learning
summary: We present gradiend, an open-source Python package that operationalizes the GRADIEND method for learning feature directions from factual-counterfactual MLM and CLM gradients in language models. The package provides a unified workflow for feature-related data creation, training, evaluation, visualization, persistent model rewriting via controlled weight updates, and multi-feature comparison. We demonstrate GRADIEND on an English pronoun paradigm and on a large-scale feature comparison that reproduces prior use cases.

AI.tldr: 介绍了一个名为gradiend的开源Python包，该包实现了GRADIEND方法，用于从语言模型中的事实-反事实MLM和CLM梯度中学习特征方向。
AI.motivation: 为了便于使用GRADIEND方法，该研究开发了一个开源工具包，以简化特征相关数据的创建、训练、评估、可视化、模型重写和多特征比较等流程。
AI.method: 该研究使用GRADIEND方法，通过计算事实-反事实MLM和CLM梯度来学习语言模型中的特征方向，并将其封装在一个名为gradiend的Python包中。
AI.result: gradiend包能够对英语代词范式进行分析，并且能够成功地在大型特征比较中复现先前的研究案例。
AI.conclusion: gradiend包提供了一个统一的工作流程，使得研究人员能够更方便地进行特征方向的学习、分析和应用，从而促进对语言模型内部机制的理解。

title: Learning to Build: Autonomous Robotic Assembly of Stable Structures Without Predefined Plans
summary: This paper presents a novel autonomous robotic assembly framework for constructing stable structures without relying on predefined architectural blueprints. Instead of following fixed plans, construction tasks are defined through targets and obstacles, allowing the system to adapt more flexibly to environmental uncertainty and variations during the building process. A reinforcement learning (RL) policy, trained using deep Q-learning with successor features, serves as the decision-making component. As a proof of concept, we evaluate the approach on a benchmark of 15 2D robotic assembly tasks of discrete block construction. Experiments using a real-world closed-loop robotic setup demonstrate the feasibility of the method and its ability to handle construction noise. The results suggest that our framework offers a promising direction for more adaptable and robust robotic construction in real-world environments.

AI.tldr: 提出了一种新颖的自主机器人装配框架，无需预定义建筑蓝图即可建造稳定结构，通过目标和障碍物定义任务，并使用深度Q学习结合后继特征的强化学习策略进行决策。在2D离散块体构建任务中进行了概念验证，并在真实机器人环境中证明了其可行性。
AI.motivation: 现有的机器人装配方法依赖于预定义的建筑蓝图，这在面对环境不确定性和变化时不够灵活。研究的动机是开发一种更具适应性和鲁棒性的机器人装配框架，能够处理现实世界中的不确定性。
AI.method: 该框架使用一种强化学习（RL）策略，具体来说是深度Q学习（DQN）结合后继特征（SF），来作为决策组件。任务不是通过固定的计划，而是通过目标和障碍物来定义，使系统能够适应环境的不确定性和变化。
AI.result: 在15个2D离散块体构建任务的基准测试中，该方法被证明是可行的。通过真实世界的闭环机器人实验，证明了该方法能够处理施工过程中的噪声，并成功构建了稳定的结构。
AI.conclusion: 所提出的自主机器人装配框架是一种有前途的研究方向，能够实现更具适应性和鲁棒性的机器人建筑，尤其是在复杂的现实世界环境中，因为它能够绕过预定义蓝图并直接通过目标和障碍物进行适应性决策。

title: EDDA-Coordinata: An Annotated Dataset of Historical Geographic Coordinates
summary: This paper introduces a dataset of enriched geographic coordinates retrieved from Diderot and d'Alembert's eighteenth-century Encyclopedie. Automatically recovering geographic coordinates from historical texts is a complex task, as they are expressed in a variety of ways and with varying levels of precision. To improve retrieval of coordinates from similar digitized early modern texts, we have created a gold standard dataset, trained models, published the resulting inferred and normalized coordinate data, and experimented applying these models to new texts. From 74,000 total articles in each of the digitized versions of the Encyclopedie from ARTFL and ENCCRE, we examined 15,278 geographical entries, manually identifying 4,798 containing coordinates, and 10,480 with descriptive but non-numerical references. Leveraging our gold standard annotations, we trained transformer-based models to retrieve and normalize coordinates. The pipeline presented here combines a classifier to identify coordinate-bearing entries and a second model for retrieval, tested across encoder-decoder and decoder architectures. Cross-validation yielded an 86% EM score. On an out-of-domain eighteenth-century Trevoux dictionary (also in French), our fine-tuned model had a 61% EM score, while for the nineteenth-century, 7th edition of the Encyclopaedia Britannica in English, the EM was 77%. These findings highlight the gold standard dataset's usefulness as training data, and our two-step method's cross-lingual, cross-domain generalizability.

AI.tldr: 该研究创建了一个包含18世纪《百科全书》地理坐标的黄金标准数据集，并训练了基于Transformer的模型来自动提取和标准化这些坐标，证明了该方法在跨语言和跨领域上的泛化能力。
AI.motivation: 从历史文本中自动提取地理坐标是一项复杂任务，因为表达方式多样且精度不一。为了提高从早期数字化文本中检索坐标的效率，研究者希望构建一个数据集并开发一个模型。
AI.method: 研究者从ARTFL和ENCCRE的《百科全书》中选取了15,278个地理条目，手动标注了4,798个包含坐标的条目，并创建了一个黄金标准数据集。利用此数据集，训练了基于Transformer的分类和检索模型，并测试了不同模型架构。
AI.result: 在《百科全书》数据集上，训练出的模型通过交叉验证获得了86%的EM得分。在跨领域测试中，该模型在18世纪的Trevoux词典（法语）上达到61%的EM得分，在19世纪的《大英百科全书》（英语）上达到77%的EM得分。
AI.conclusion: 该研究提出的黄金标准数据集对于训练模型非常有用，并且所开发的双步方法在跨语言和跨领域上都具有良好的泛化能力，能够有效地从历史文献中提取地理坐标。

title: EgoGraph: Temporal Knowledge Graph for Egocentric Video Understanding
summary: Ultra-long egocentric videos spanning multiple days present significant challenges for video understanding. Existing approaches still rely on fragmented local processing and limited temporal modeling, restricting their ability to reason over such extended sequences. To address these limitations, we introduce EgoGraph, a training-free and dynamic knowledge-graph construction framework that explicitly encodes long-term, cross-entity dependencies in egocentric video streams. EgoGraph employs a novel egocentric schema that unifies the extraction and abstraction of core entities, such as people, objects, locations, and events, and structurally reasons about their attributes and interactions, yielding a significantly richer and more coherent semantic representation than traditional clip-based video models. Crucially, we develop a temporal relational modeling strategy that captures temporal dependencies across entities and accumulates stable long-term memory over multiple days, enabling complex temporal reasoning. Extensive experiments on the EgoLifeQA and EgoR1-bench benchmarks demonstrate that EgoGraph achieves state-of-the-art performance on long-term video question answering, validating its effectiveness as a new paradigm for ultra-long egocentric video understanding.

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为 EgoGraph 的新框架，用于处理超长、以自我为中心的视频。该框架通过构建动态知识图谱来捕捉实体间的长期依赖关系和时间演化，解决了现有方法局部处理和时间建模能力不足的问题。
AI.motivation: 现有的以自我为中心的视频理解方法在处理跨越数天的超长视频时存在局限性，它们依赖于零散的局部处理和有限的时间建模，难以进行长期的推理。
AI.method: EgoGraph 框架通过以下方式运作：1. 引入一种新颖的以自我为中心模式，用于统一提取和抽象核心实体（人物、物体、地点、事件）。2. 构建动态知识图谱，显式编码实体间的长期、跨实体依赖关系，并推理其属性和交互。3. 开发一种时间关系建模策略，捕捉实体间的时间依赖性，并积累稳定的长期记忆。
AI.result: EgoGraph 在 EgoLifeQA 和 EgoR1-bench 基准测试中取得了最先进的性能，特别是在长期视频问答任务上，有效验证了其处理超长以自我为中心视频的有效性。
AI.conclusion: EgoGraph 提出了一种新的超长以自我为中心视频理解范式，通过动态知识图谱的构建和时间关系建模，能够更有效地理解和推理超长视频中的信息。

title: Dialect and Gender Bias in YouTube's Spanish Captioning System
summary: Spanish is the official language of twenty-one countries and is spoken by over 441 million people. Naturally, there are many variations in how Spanish is spoken across these countries. Media platforms such as YouTube rely on automatic speech recognition systems to make their content accessible to different groups of users. However, YouTube offers only one option for automatically generating captions in Spanish. This raises the question: could this captioning system be biased against certain Spanish dialects? This study examines the potential biases in YouTube's automatic captioning system by analyzing its performance across various Spanish dialects. By comparing the quality of captions for female and male speakers from different regions, we identify systematic disparities which can be attributed to specific dialects. Our study provides further evidence that algorithmic technologies deployed on digital platforms need to be calibrated to the diverse needs and experiences of their user populations.

AI.tldr: 该研究分析了YouTube自动西班牙语字幕在不同西班牙语方言之间的表现差异，发现其存在偏见，并强调了技术平台需要根据用户多样性进行调整。
AI.motivation: YouTube的西班牙语自动字幕系统只提供一种通用选项，可能对某些西班牙语方言存在偏见，影响了内容的可用性。
AI.method: 通过分析来自不同地区、不同性别发言者的YouTube自动字幕质量，比较不同西班牙语方言的表现。
AI.result: 研究发现了YouTube自动字幕在不同西班牙语方言之间存在系统性差异，这些差异可归因于特定方言。
AI.conclusion: 算法技术在数字平台上的部署需要针对用户群体的多样化需求和体验进行校准，以避免偏见。

title: Enhancing Vision-Language Navigation with Multimodal Event Knowledge from Real-World Indoor Tour Videos
summary: Vision-Language Navigation (VLN) agents often struggle with long-horizon reasoning in unseen environments, particularly when facing ambiguous, coarse-grained instructions. While recent advances use knowledge graph to enhance reasoning, the potential of multimodal event knowledge inspired by human episodic memory remains underexplored. In this work, we propose an event-centric knowledge enhancement strategy for automated process knowledge mining and feature fusion to solve coarse-grained instruction and long-horizon reasoning in VLN task. First, we construct YE-KG, the first large-scale multimodal spatiotemporal knowledge graph, with over 86k nodes and 83k edges, derived from real-world indoor videos. By leveraging multimodal large language models (i.e., LLaVa, GPT4), we extract unstructured video streams into structured semantic-action-effect events to serve as explicit episodic memory. Second, we introduce STE-VLN, which integrates the above graph into VLN models via a Coarse-to-Fine Hierarchical Retrieval mechanism. This allows agents to retrieve causal event sequences and dynamically fuse them with egocentric visual observations. Experiments on REVERIE, R2R, and R2R-CE benchmarks demonstrate the efficiency of our event-centric strategy, outperforming state-of-the-art approaches across diverse action spaces. Our data and code are available on the project website https://sites.google.com/view/y-event-kg/.

AI.tldr: 本研究提出了一种基于事件知识图谱的视觉语言导航（VLN）方法，通过构建大规模多模态时空知识图谱（YE-KG）并利用其进行事件序列检索和融合，以解决长指令和未见环境下的导航问题。
AI.motivation: 现有的VLN方法在处理长指令和未知环境下的长距离推理时存在困难，特别是当指令模糊时。尽管知识图谱有所应用，但受人类情景记忆启发的多模态事件知识的潜力尚未被充分挖掘。
AI.method: 研究者构建了一个名为YE-KG的大规模多模态时空知识图谱，其中包含8.6万个节点和8.3万条边，来源于真实室内视频。利用多模态大语言模型（LLaVa, GPT4）将非结构化视频流提取为结构化的语义-动作-效应事件，作为显式的情景记忆。随后，提出STE-VLN模型，通过“粗粒度到细粒度”的层次化检索机制将YE-KG集成到VLN模型中，以检索因果事件序列并动态融合这些事件与代理的视觉观察。
AI.result: 在REVERIE、R2R和R2R-CE数据集上的实验表明，所提出的事件中心策略在各种动作空间下都优于现有最先进的方法。
AI.conclusion: 基于事件知识图谱的方法能够有效地增强VLN代理在处理粗粒度指令和进行长距离推理时的能力，尤其是在未知环境中。

title: Learning Robust Control Policies for Inverted Pose on Miniature Blimp Robots
summary: The ability to achieve and maintain inverted poses is essential for unlocking the full agility of miniature blimp robots (MBRs). However, developing reliable control methods for MBRs remains challenging due to their complex and underactuated dynamics. To address this challenge, we propose a novel framework that enables robust control policy learning for inverted pose on MBRs. The proposed framework operates through three core stages: First, a high-fidelity three-dimensional (3D) simulation environment was constructed, which was calibrated against real-world MBR motion data to ensure accurate replication of inverted-state dynamics. Second, a robust policy for MBR inverted control was trained within the simulation environment via a domain randomization strategy and a modified Twin Delayed Deep Deterministic Policy Gradient (TD3) algorithm. Third, a mapping layer was designed to bridge the sim-to-real gap for the learned policy deployment. Comprehensive evaluations in the simulation environment demonstrate that the learned policy achieves a higher success rate compared to the energy-shaping controller. Furthermore, experimental results confirm that the learned policy with a mapping layer enables an MBR to achieve and maintain a fully upside-down pose in real-world settings.

AI.tldr: 研究提出了一种新颖的框架，通过高保真仿真、领域随机化和改进的TD3算法来训练控制策略，并设计了一个映射层来实现机器人模拟到现实的部署，成功使微型飞艇机器人在现实世界中实现并维持倒立姿态。
AI.motivation: 微型飞艇机器人（MBRs）要实现完全的敏捷性，需要能够实现和维持倒立姿态，但由于其复杂的欠驱动动力学，开发可靠的控制方法具有挑战性。
AI.method:
1. 构建了高保真的三维仿真环境，并使用真实世界MBR运动数据进行校准。
2. 在仿真环境中，通过领域随机化策略和修改后的Twin Delayed Deep Deterministic Policy Gradient（TD3）算法，训练了MBR倒立控制的鲁棒策略。
3. 设计了一个映射层来弥合模拟到现实的差距，以便部署学习到的策略。
AI.result: 在仿真环境中，学习到的策略比能量塑形控制器具有更高的成功率。在现实世界的实验中，带有映射层的学习策略成功使MBR实现了并维持了完全的倒立姿态。
AI.conclusion: 所提出的框架能够通过仿真和学习策略，成功使MBR在现实世界中实现并维持倒立姿态，解决了现有控制方法的挑战。

title: Can Unified Generation and Understanding Models Maintain Semantic Equivalence Across Different Output Modalities?
summary: Unified Multimodal Large Language Models (U-MLLMs) integrate understanding and generation within a single architecture. However, existing evaluations typically assess these capabilities separately, overlooking semantic equivalence, i.e., the ability to manifest consistent reasoning results regardless of the output modality. In this work, we investigate whether current U-MLLMs satisfy this premise. We observe that while models demonstrate robust textual reasoning, they fail to maintain semantic equivalence when required to render the same results in the image modality. To rigorously diagnose this discrepancy, we introduce VGUBench, a framework to decouple reasoning logic from generation fidelity. VGUBench comprises three diagnostic tasks: (1)Textual Generative Understanding, establishing a baseline for reasoning accuracy in textual response; (2)Visual Generative Understanding, evaluating the ability to generate visual responses that represent the correct answer; and (3)a Visual Rendering control task, which assesses the ability to directly render explicit visual descriptions into images without complex reasoning. Our evaluation reveals a significant disparity: despite strong performance in textual understanding and visual rendering, U-MLLMs exhibit a marked performance collapse when required to generate visual answers to questions. Furthermore, we find a negligible correlation between visual answering performance and basic rendering quality. These results suggest that the failure stems not from insufficient generation fidelity, but from a breakdown in cross-modal semantic alignment. We provide diagnostic insights to address this challenge in future Unified Generation and Understanding Models.

AI.tldr: 本文通过引入VGUBench框架，评估了统一多模态大语言模型（U-MLLMs）在不同模态下进行推理的语义一致性。研究发现，现有模型在文本推理方面表现良好，但在生成图像结果时存在显著的性能下降，这表明问题在于跨模态语义对齐的失效，而非生成能力不足。
AI.motivation: 现有U-MLLMs的评估方法将理解和生成能力分开，忽略了在不同输出模态下保持推理结果语义一致性的问题。本文旨在探究当前U-MLLMs是否满足这种语义一致性。
AI.method: 作者提出了VGUBench评估框架，包含三个诊断任务：1. 文本生成理解（TGU），评估文本推理准确性；2. 视觉生成理解（VGU），评估生成正确答案的图像能力；3. 视觉渲染控制（VRC），评估直接将视觉描述渲染成图像的能力。通过在这些任务上评估现有U-MLLMs来诊断问题。
AI.result: U-MLLMs在文本理解和视觉渲染方面表现强劲，但在需要生成视觉答案时性能显著下降。视觉回答性能与基本的渲染质量之间几乎没有相关性。
AI.conclusion: U-MLLMs在跨模态语义对齐方面存在问题，导致在生成视觉结果时出现性能衰退，这并非由于生成保真度不足。研究为未来统一生成和理解模型的设计提供了诊断见解。

title: LemmaBench: A Live, Research-Level Benchmark to Evaluate LLM Capabilities in Mathematics
summary: We present a new approach for benchmarking Large Language Model (LLM) capabilities on research-level mathematics. Existing benchmarks largely rely on static, hand-curated sets of contest or textbook-style problems as proxies for mathematical research. Instead, we establish an updatable benchmark evaluating models directly on the latest research results in mathematics. This consists of an automatic pipeline that extracts lemmas from arXiv and rewrites them into self-contained statements by making all assumptions and required definitions explicit. It results in a benchmark that can be updated regularly with new problems taken directly from human mathematical research, while previous instances can be used for training without compromising future evaluations. We benchmark current state-of-the-art LLMs, which obtain around 10-15$\%$ accuracy in theorem proving (pass@1) depending on the model, showing that there is currently a large margin of progression for LLMs to reach human-level proving capabilities in a research context.

AI.tldr: 该研究提出了一种新的、可更新的基准测试方法，用于评估大型语言模型（LLM）在研究级数学方面的能力，通过直接从 arXiv 提取和改写最新的数学研究论文中的引理来构建，结果表明当前最先进的 LLM 在定理证明方面的准确率约为 10-15%，与人类水平相比仍有很大差距。
AI.motivation: 现有的数学能力基准测试主要依赖于静态的、手工 curated 的竞赛或教科书式问题，无法准确反映 LLM 在前沿数学研究中的实际能力。因此，需要一种能够直接评估模型在最新数学研究成果上的能力，并且可以持续更新的基准测试方法。
AI.method: 研究构建了一个自动化的流程，该流程从 arXiv 上的数学论文中提取引理，并将其改写成自包含的陈述，明确列出所有假设和必要的定义。这种方法能够定期用来自人类数学研究的新问题更新基准，同时允许将旧问题用于训练，而不影响未来的评估。
